author: @rhread
text: I doubt it is only 250 watts? 36 volts X 12 amp controller = 432 watts. A 12 amp brushless controller is the smallest I’ve ever seen for an E bike. I would be interested to see the amperage rating for the controller. Thank you again for the video

author: @huckmiddeke665
text:
A thought:
Looking at "what is enough?" to sustain (also human) life on earth, I wonder where the energy and resources for charging, creating, making and using this bike come from. Can we, as a species, justify this expense? Is it ultimately supportive for the survival of our species?
Let's look at it as an investment. 
An experiment?
Finding the sweet spot between "nice to have" and "need to have", I think that ebikes can get people out of vehicles which are heavier than the freight.
Is "vehicle weight" to "freight weight" ratio interesting to look at when exploring high level sustainability?
[Deep nature connection certainly is.]

How long (time/distance) do you [Luke?!] need to (stationary?) pedal to charge how many kilometers into the bike?
How big is the detour?
Building anything that requires new resources [incl infra] is unable to be sustained as an approach.
So what to do with this bike? Why do we need it?
I am thinking that ebikes with rearhub motor are more multipurpose. How to add weights "when arriving at camp" to turn the wheel into a flywheel? Water filled innertubes interwoven with the spokes?
What other add-ons? A mobile dynapod? 
Connectable to modular systems (m10 or m12?) and to other mobile dynapods? Running an array of fly wheels on one axle to power a welder? 
The bike would certainly need a spoon holder.

replies.author: @huckmiddeke665
replies.text:
And on another thought:
You have shown (thank you) that ebikes aren't black magic to DIY. I am however wondering if they shouldn't have factory setting that allows them to be easily switched to  electricity generation?

author: @TechKnowCSS
text: AWFUL NICE OF YOU TO DELETE MY COMMENT MATE.......

replies.author: @matthewellisor5835
replies.text:
Easy now neighbor, perhaps don't come in with steam at full head.
We all know that the YouTube algorithm too often cans comments on a false positive. 
If you believe that your comment might have encroached on Rob's clearly established rules, wouldn't it be better to instead apologize for any perceived slight (even if you don't know what it is), state that it wasn't intended to cause conflict and have an ask of exactly what you should do going forward?
Just something to consider.

HAD NO WEBSITE LINKS NOR ANY FOUL LANGUAGE OR  DEROGATIVE REMARKS TO ANYONE OR ANY THING. SO IN THAT FRAMEWORK WOULD YOU NOT SAY ANYTHING?

author: @scotttovey
text:
You can't have a throttle controller on it?

What are you supposed to do, just run people over when you approach a crowd and can't slow down or stop because there's no throttle control on the thing?

I am no doubt seeing that from an ignorance of E-bike technology perspective, but it still strikes me as rather stupid.

replies.author: @cwt5654
replies.text: Sensor on the chainwheel cuts power as soon as  you stop pedalling... also there is (usually) a cutout cable on the brake levers which activates when pressure is applied.

replies.author: @matthewellisor5835
replies.text:
CTW 56 is correct. 
A big problem I see is the ultra-crepidarian legislators too busy getting paid-off by lobbying groups and speaking from the South-facing end of a North-bound mule to bother will petty little things like learning about the topics or, heaven forbid, reading and understanding to what they put their pen.
